Whoever loves the Lord should, for His sake, have fixed their love upon one of the Lord's people. Solomon was a wise man, a rich man, a great man; yet the best praise of him is that which characterizes all saints, even the poorest: “He loved the Lord.” Where God sows abundantly, He expects to reap accordingly; and those who truly love God and His worship will not begrudge the expenses of their religion. We must never think what is spent for the service of God is wasted.

Solomon's dream was not a common one. While his physical abilities were locked up in sleep, the powers of his soul were strengthened; he was enabled to receive the Divine vision and make a suitable choice. God puts us in the ready way to be happy by assuring us we will have what we need and pray for. Solomon’s making such a choice while asleep, when the powers of reason are least active, showed it came from the grace of God. Having a humble sense of his own wants and weakness, he pleads, Lord, I am but a little child. The more wise and considerate men are, the better acquainted they are with their own weakness and the more jealous of themselves. Solomon asks God to give him wisdom. We must pray for it so that it may help us in our particular calling and the various occasions we have. Those who prefer spiritual blessings to earthly good are accepted by God. It was a prevailing prayer, and prevailed for more than he asked. God gave him wisdom, such as no other prince was ever blessed with; and also gave him riches and honor. If we secure wisdom and grace, these will bring outward prosperity with them or sweeten the lack of it. The way to get spiritual blessings is to wrestle with God in prayer for them. The way to get earthly blessings is to refer ourselves to God concerning them. Solomon has wisdom given him because he did ask it, and wealth because he did not.

An instance of Solomon’s wisdom is given. Notice the difficulty of the case. To find out the true mother, he could not try which the child loved best, so he tried which loved the child best: the mother's sincerity will be tested when the child is in danger. Parents should show their love to their children, especially by taking care of their souls and snatching them as brands out of the burning. By this and other instances of the wisdom with which God endued him, Solomon had great reputation among his people. This was better to him than weapons of war; for this he was both feared and loved.
